tasks.name: 🔐 Role-Based Access Control & Granular Data Permissions
tasks.notes:
🔴 HIGH PRIORITY — SECURITY

# Role-Based Access Control & Granular Data Permissions

## Overview
Build a comprehensive permission system that:
1. Controls WHO can do WHAT (feature-level permissions)
2. Controls WHO can see WHICH DATA (data-level access control)
3. Uses permission groups (role templates) like QuickBooks — predefined bundles of permissions that admins can customize
4. Integrates deeply with the AI chat system so Claude tells users exactly what they CAN'T access when they hit a boundary
5. Allows admins to grant/deny individual permissions within groups

## Current State (Basic)
- 4 hardcoded roles: VIEWER, CREATOR, POWER_USER, ADMIN
- Roles checked in session.ts helper functions (canCreateDashboard, canEditGlossary, canAccessSensitiveData)
- No granular data-source-level access control
- AI system prompt has no concept of user permissions — it can suggest any metric to anyone

## Target State (Granular)

### Permission Groups (Role Templates)
Predefined bundles like QuickBooks. Admins can create custom groups or override individual permissions within a group.

| Group Template       | Feature Access                         | Data Categories Allowed                    |
|---------------------|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| **Viewer**          | View shared dashboards only            | None (only sees pre-built dashboards)      |
| **Dept. Analyst**   | Create dashboards, use widget library  | Own department category only               |
| **Cross-Func Analyst** | Create, publish widgets, share     | Multiple categories (admin-assigned)       |
| **Power User**      | Full creation, templates, publishing   | All data sources                           |
| **Admin**           | Everything + user mgmt, glossary, permissions | Everything                          |

### Data Categories (mapped from glossary + data sources)
- **Revenue**: MRR, ARR, NRR, LTV, ARPU, CAC, Gross Margin, Expansion/Contraction Revenue, CAC Payback
- **Retention**: Churn Rate, Retention Rate, GRR
- **Support**: FRT, Resolution Time, CSAT, NPS, Ticket Backlog
- **Sales**: Win Rate, Pipeline Value, Avg Deal Size, Sales Cycle, Lead Conversion, Quota Attainment
- **Product**: Daily Active Usage, Feature Adoption, TTV, Onboarding Completion
- **Operations**: Headcount, Revenue Per Employee, Burn Rate, Runway
- **Customer PII**: Individual customer names, emails, companies (separate toggle)

### Data Source Mapping
Each data source table maps to one or more categories:
- sample_customers → Customer PII + general
- sample_subscriptions → Revenue + Retention
- sample_tickets → Support
- sample_revenue → Revenue + Operations
- sample_usage → Product
- sample_deals → Sales

### AI Chat Integration (Critical)
When a user asks the AI for something they can't access:
- AI must know the user's allowed/denied data categories BEFORE generating patches
- System prompt includes a "RESTRICTED DATA" section listing what the user CANNOT access
- AI responds with a friendly denial: "You don't have access to [Revenue data / ARR / etc.]. Please contact your administrator to request access."
- AI suggests ALTERNATIVE metrics the user CAN see
- Widget library panel only shows widgets the user's permissions allow

### Permission Check Flow
1. User sends chat message → API resolves effective permissions
2. Permissions injected into buildSystemPrompt() as allowed/denied lists
3. AI generates schema patches → schema patcher validates data source access
4. queryData() checks permissions before returning data
5. Widget renderers show "Access Denied" placeholder for unauthorized widgets

### Admin UI
- Permission Groups page: list, create, edit, delete groups
- Each group: checkboxes for feature permissions + data category toggles
- User Management page: assign group to user, override individual permissions
- Audit log: track permission changes

## Database Schema Changes
- PermissionGroup: id, name, description, isSystem, featurePermissions (JSON), dataPermissions (JSON)
- UserPermissionAssignment: userId, permissionGroupId, customOverrides (JSON), assignedBy, assignedAt
- DataAccessRule: permissionGroupId, dataCategory, accessLevel (FULL/NONE/FILTERED)
- AuditLog entries for all permission changes

## Key Files to Modify
- prisma/schema.prisma — new models
- src/lib/auth/session.ts — expand with permission resolution
- src/lib/auth/permissions.ts — NEW: permission checking engine
- src/lib/ai/prompts.ts — inject user permissions into system prompt
- src/lib/data/sample-data.ts — permission-gated queryData()
- src/app/api/chat/route.ts — resolve permissions before calling Claude
- src/components/dashboard/DashboardCanvas.tsx — permission-aware widget rendering
- src/components/widgets/WidgetLibraryPanel.tsx — filter by accessible data
- src/app/admin/permissions/ — NEW: admin UI pages

## Security Principles
- Deny by default — if no explicit permission, access is denied
- Server-side enforcement — never trust client-side permission checks alone
- Principle of least privilege — new users get Viewer permissions
- Audit everything — every permission grant/revoke is logged
- AI is permission-aware — the LLM never suggests metrics the user can't see

tasks.name: 🔴 SECURITY PRIORITY: Granular Data-Level Permissions Before Wider Rollout
tasks.notes:
⚠️ BLOCKING: This must be completed before opening InsightHub to non-admin users.

## Why This Is Critical
InsightHub will allow anyone in the company to build dashboards. Without granular data permissions:
- A Sales rep could see Burn Rate, Runway, and other sensitive Operations data
- A Support agent could see individual deal values and pipeline data
- Anyone could access Customer PII (emails, names, companies)
- The AI assistant would happily help them build widgets with ANY data — it has no concept of access restrictions

## What We're Building
A QuickBooks-style permission system where:
1. **Permission Groups** = predefined role templates (Viewer, Dept Analyst, Power User, Admin)
2. **Data Categories** = Revenue, Retention, Support, Sales, Product, Operations, Customer PII
3. Each group defines which data categories are accessible
4. Admins can customize individual permissions within groups (grant or deny any specific permission)
5. **AI is permission-aware** — Claude tells users exactly what they can't access and suggests alternatives

## Key Deliverable
When a Support analyst asks Claude "show me ARR by month", the AI responds:
> "You don't have access to Revenue data (which includes ARR, MRR, NRR, LTV). Please contact your administrator to request access. I can help you with Support metrics like FRT, Resolution Time, and CSAT instead — would you like a dashboard with those?"

## Parent Task
All implementation details are in: 🔐 Role-Based Access Control & Granular Data Permissions (11 subtasks)

## Timeline
This should be completed BEFORE any broader company rollout.

tasks.name: 🤖 AI-Assisted SQL & Query Explanation
tasks.notes:
Make Claude a SQL copilot for power users. Not just dashboard building — actual query assistance.

## Features

### "Explain This Query" in Chat
- Paste any SQL into the chat → Claude explains it in plain English
- Breaks down: what tables, what joins, what filters, what the result represents
- Maps query components to glossary terms ("This SUM(monthly_amount) is your MRR")
- Highlights potential issues: missing WHERE clauses, Cartesian joins, etc.

### Natural Language → SQL
- User types: "Show me churn rate by region for enterprise customers last quarter"
- Claude generates the SQL query (not just a widget — the actual SQL)
- Shows the query in a code block with "Run in SQL Editor" / "Run in Playground" buttons
- Respects the user's permission level (only generates queries for allowed data sources)

### SQL → Optimized SQL
- Paste a slow query → Claude suggests optimizations
- Snowflake-specific: clustering keys, materialized views, QUALIFY instead of subquery, etc.
- Shows before/after with explanation of why the optimized version is better

### "Verify My Dashboard" Mode
- User says: "I want to verify the MRR number on this dashboard"
- Claude generates the exact Snowflake SQL that would reproduce the widget's value
- Includes instructions: "Run this in your Snowflake worksheet to cross-check"
- Highlights any differences between the InsightHub query and what a raw Snowflake query would look like

### Formula Help (for Visual Builder users)
- "How do I calculate net revenue retention?"
- Claude responds with both:
  - The Sigma-style formula: `(Sum([MRR_end]) - Sum([MRR_new])) / Sum([MRR_start]) * 100`
  - The SQL equivalent
  - The official glossary definition
  - A "Use this formula" button that inserts it into the formula bar

## Prompt Engineering
- New system prompt section: "SQL Assistant Mode"
- When user's message contains SQL or asks for a query, switch to SQL-focused response format
- Include Snowflake dialect reference (key functions, syntax differences from standard SQL)
- Include the user's schema metadata so Claude can write accurate queries

## Files
- src/lib/ai/prompts.ts — add SQL assistant prompt section
- src/app/api/chat/route.ts — detect SQL-mode messages
- New response format for SQL results (code block + action buttons)

tasks.name: 📊 Responsive Widget Rendering — Charts & Tables on All Screen Sizes
tasks.notes:
Widgets (KPI cards, charts, tables) must render beautifully at all sizes from phone to ultrawide desktop.

## Current State
- Recharts ResponsiveContainer is used (auto-resizes to parent) — good foundation
- Tables have no horizontal scroll handling
- KPI cards have fixed-ish layouts

## Needed

### Charts (Recharts)
- Small viewports: hide axis labels, reduce tick count, increase font size
- Phone: simplify to sparkline or mini-chart (no axis, no legend, just the shape)
- Tablet: full chart but with abbreviated labels
- Desktop: full detail ✅
- Touch: tooltip on tap (not hover) — Recharts supports this via `onMouseMove` but needs touch event pass-through

### Tables
- Phone: horizontal scroll with sticky first column
- Tablet: show all columns if they fit, scroll if not
- Desktop: full table ✅
- Add swipe indicator (shadow on right edge) when content is scrollable

### KPI Cards
- Phone: stack horizontally in a scrollable row, or 2-column mini grid
- Tablet: 2-3 across ✅
- Desktop: as designed ✅
- Ensure trend arrows and percentages are readable at small sizes

### Text Blocks / Dividers
- These scale naturally with text reflow ✅
- Just ensure padding/margins adjust on smaller screens

### Gauges / Funnels
- SVG-based: already scale, but verify they don't overflow on small containers
- Minimum size: set a sensible min-height so they don't become unreadable

## Files
- src/components/dashboard/widgets/ — all widget renderers
- Recharts config: adjust tick count, font size, legend position based on container width
- Use container queries (CSS) or ResizeObserver for per-widget responsiveness

tasks.name: ⌨️ Keyboard Shortcut System — ? Overlay, Vim-Style Navigation & Command Palette
tasks.notes:
⌨️ Keyboard Shortcut System — Phase 2

A comprehensive, thoughtful keyboard shortcut experience inspired by GitHub (? overlay), Figma (spacebar pan), Linear (c to create), and our own AutoQA review inbox (j/k navigation, number keys for tabs, ? help overlay, platform-aware ⌘/Ctrl display).

## Design Philosophy
- **Discoverable**: ? overlay teaches shortcuts. Tooltips show shortcuts on hover.
- **Vim-inspired navigation**: j/k for next/prev, g+key for "go to" commands
- **Single-key where possible**: No modifier keys for the most common actions
- **Platform-aware**: ⌘ on Mac, Ctrl on Windows — detected via navigator.platform, toggled via CSS class (is-mac / is-win pattern from AutoQA)
- **Never hijack inputs**: All single-key shortcuts disabled when focus is in an input/textarea
- **Context-sensitive**: Different shortcuts active on different pages (editor vs gallery vs landing)
- **Escape always works**: Closes any overlay, blurs inputs, clears focus

## Shortcut Reference (The Full List)

### ? Help Overlay (available everywhere)
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| ? (Shift+/) | Toggle keyboard shortcuts overlay |
| Esc | Close overlay / close any modal / blur input |

### Global Navigation (available on all pages)
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| g then h | Go to Home (landing page) |
| g then d | Go to Dashboards (gallery) |
| g then g | Go to Glossary |
| g then n | Go to New Dashboard |
| g then s | Go to Settings |
| ⌘K / Ctrl+K | Open command palette (spotlight search) |
| / | Focus the nearest search/input field |

### Dashboard Editor
| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| ⌘S / Ctrl+S | Save dashboard |
| ⌘Z / Ctrl+Z | Undo |
| ⌘⇧Z / Ctrl+⇧Z | Redo |
| / | Focus chat input |
| c | Toggle chat panel (open/close drawer on narrow viewports) |
| w | Toggle widget library panel |
| Esc | Close any open panel / deselect widget / cancel drag |
| Delete / Backspace | Delete selected widget |
| ⌘D / Ctrl+D | Duplicate selected widget |
| ↑ ↓ ← → | Nudge selected widget by 1 grid cell |
| ⇧ + arrow | Resize selected widget (expand/shrink in that direction) |
| Tab | Cycle focus through widgets |
| ⇧Tab | Cycle focus backwards |
| Enter | Open selected widget's edit/config panel |
| 1-9 | Quick-select widget by position (first 9 widgets) |
| f | Toggle fullscreen for selected widget |
| p | Toggle preview mode (hide grid lines, show final look) |
| t | Add text block at next available position |
| v | Toggle version timeline |
| ⌘⇧P / Ctrl+⇧P | Open command palette within editor |

### Dashboard Viewing (read-only dashboards, shared views)

| j / k | Scroll to next / previous widget |

### Gallery Page

## Implementation

### Architecture
- `src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts` — expand existing hook with full shortcut registry
- `src/hooks/usePlatform.ts` — NEW: platform detection hook
- `src/components/ui/KeyboardShortcutsOverlay.tsx` — NEW: ? overlay modal
- `src/components/ui/Kbd.tsx` — NEW: styled <kbd> component (platform-aware)
- `src/lib/keyboard/shortcut-registry.ts` — NEW: declarative shortcut definitions with context scoping

### Shortcut Registry Pattern
```typescript
const shortcuts: ShortcutDef[] = [
  { key: '?', description: 'Show keyboard shortcuts', scope: 'global', action: toggleOverlay },
  { key: 'g h', description: 'Go to Home', scope: 'global', action: () => router.push('/') },
  { key: 'mod+s', description: 'Save dashboard', scope: 'editor', action: save },
  // mod = ⌘ on Mac, Ctrl on Windows
];
```

### g-prefix "Go To" Commands (GitHub-style)
Two-key sequence: press g, then within 500ms press the second key.
Visual indicator: after pressing g, show a subtle "g..." toast in bottom-left corner.
If no second key within 500ms, the g is ignored.

### Platform-Aware Display
Same CSS pattern as AutoQA:
```css
html.is-mac .win-keys { display: none !important; }
html.is-win .mac-keys { display: none !important; }
```
Detection: `const isMac = /Mac|iPhone|iPad|iPod/i.test(navigator.platform || navigator.userAgent);`

### ? Overlay Design
- Glassmorphism panel matching InsightHub dark theme
- Sections: Global, Editor, Viewing, Gallery, Glossary
- Each row: description (left) — keys (right)
- <kbd> elements styled like physical keys (subtle shadow, rounded, mono font)
- Platform-aware: show ⌘ on Mac, Ctrl on Windows (dual rendering with CSS toggle)
- Animate in: backdrop blur + scale-up from center
- Close on: Esc, ?, click outside
- Footer: "Tip: Most shortcuts work only outside text inputs"

### Tooltip Integration
Every toolbar button, nav item, and action button should show its shortcut in the tooltip:
- "Save (⌘S)" / "Save (Ctrl+S)"
- "Undo (⌘Z)" / "Widget Library (W)"
- Use the Kbd component inline in tooltips

### Command Palette (⌘K)
A spotlight-style search that also surfaces keyboard actions:
- Search across: dashboards, glossary terms, recent actions, keyboard commands
- Shows matching shortcuts next to each result
- "Type > to search commands" (like VS Code)
- Fuzzy match

tasks.name: 📱 Responsive Editor Layout — Adaptive Canvas + Chat
tasks.notes:
The dashboard editor (canvas + chat + widget library) needs to adapt to viewport size. Currently it's a rigid side-by-side layout that breaks below ~900px.

## Breakpoint Strategy

### Desktop Wide (≥ 1280px) — Current behavior
- Canvas takes remaining space, chat panel fixed on right (resizable via ResizableDivider)
- Widget library slides in from left
- Version timeline at bottom of chat
- Everything works as-is ✅

### Desktop Narrow (1024px – 1279px)
- Chat panel narrows to minimum 280px, canvas gets more space
- Widget library overlays instead of pushing canvas
- Version timeline collapses to icon row (expand on click)

### Tablet Landscape (768px – 1023px)
- Chat becomes a slide-over drawer from right (not fixed panel)
- Floating chat toggle button (bottom-right, like a chat bubble)
- Canvas gets full width when chat is closed
- Widget library is a modal sheet instead of side panel
- Context menu stays the same (right-click or long-press)

### Tablet Portrait / Large Phone (640px – 767px)
- Two-mode toggle: "Canvas" view and "Chat" view (full screen each)
- Swipe gesture to switch between them, or tab bar at bottom
- Canvas widgets stack to 1-2 columns
- Toolbar simplifies (icons only, no labels)

### Phone (< 640px)
- Chat-first experience: chat is the default view
- "View Dashboard" button shows a scrollable single-column widget stack
- Dashboard is VIEW-ONLY on phone (no drag, no resize, no context menu)
- Gallery and landing page fully optimized
- Bottom nav bar replaces top navbar

## Implementation Approach
- CSS-first where possible (Tailwind responsive classes)
- useViewport() hook for JS-dependent layout switches
- React context for layout mode: 'desktop' | 'tablet' | 'mobile'
- Avoid CSS media queries in JS — use ResizeObserver for container queries

tasks.name: 🖥️ Desktop Polish — Mac & Windows Browser Consistency
tasks.notes:
PRIORITY 1 — The desktop experience must be flawlessly elegant on both Mac and Windows.

## Cross-Browser Matrix
Test and fix across:
| Browser | Mac | Windows |
|---------|-----|---------|
| Chrome  | ✅ Primary | ✅ Primary |
| Safari  | ✅ Important | N/A |
| Firefox | ✅ Test | ✅ Test |
| Edge    | N/A | ✅ Test |

## Known Platform Differences to Address

### Scrollbar Behavior
- **Mac**: Scrollbars auto-hide, only appear on scroll (overlay style)
- **Windows**: Scrollbars always visible, take up layout space (16px wide by default)
- Current: WebKit-only custom scrollbar in globals.css, Firefox gets nothing
- Fix: Add Firefox scrollbar styling (`scrollbar-width: thin; scrollbar-color`), test that layouts don't shift when scrollbar appears on Windows

### Font Rendering
- **Mac**: Subpixel antialiasing, fonts appear slightly heavier
- **Windows**: ClearType rendering, can look thinner
- Test: Ensure glassmorphism text remains readable on both platforms at all font weights
- Consider: `-webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased` (already default in Tailwind v4, verify)

### Keyboard Shortcuts
- Current: hardcoded ⌘ symbols everywhere
- Fix: Detect platform, show ⌘ on Mac and Ctrl on Windows
- Affected: useKeyboardShortcuts.ts, any tooltip/UI showing shortcuts
- Create a `usePlatform()` hook that returns `{ isMac, modKey: '⌘' | 'Ctrl' }`

### Backdrop Filter (Glassmorphism)
- Safari: Needs `-webkit-backdrop-filter` (already present in globals.css ✅)
- Firefox: `backdrop-filter` supported since Firefox 103, but verify older versions degrade gracefully
- Fallback: Solid semi-transparent background for browsers without backdrop-filter support

### Window Resize
- Editor layout must handle window resize gracefully
- ResizableDivider already exists, but the overall layout should reflow if window gets narrow
- At ~1024px width: consider auto-collapsing chat panel to an overlay
- At ~768px: switch to mobile layout

### Focus / Active States
- Windows: Default focus ring is very visible (blue outline)
- Ensure all interactive elements have custom focus-visible styles that match the glassmorphism aesthetic
- Never remove focus indicators — just style them consistently

### Context Menu
- Right-click context menu works identically across platforms ✅
- Verify positioning near screen edges (especially right edge on Windows with visible scrollbar)

## Files to Audit
- src/app/globals.css — scrollbar, font rendering, backdrop fallbacks
- src/hooks/useKeyboardShortcuts.ts — platform detection for shortcut display
- src/components/layout/ResizableDivider.tsx — window resize behavior
- src/components/dashboard/ContextMenu.tsx — edge positioning
- All tooltip/shortcut UI text — ⌘ vs Ctrl

🖥️📱 Cross-Platform Optimization Epic

## Priority Order
1. **Desktop (Mac + Windows)** — MUST be flawlessly elegant. This is the primary experience.
2. **Tablet (iPad)** — Important secondary. Dashboard viewing & light editing should feel native.
3. **Mobile (iPhone, Android)** — Dashboard viewing, chat interaction, gallery browsing. Not full editing.

## Current State
- MobileNotice.tsx shows a "use desktop" banner on < 768px — that's it
- Editor layout: fixed `h-screen flex` with 340px hardcoded chat width, zero responsive behavior
- No touch events — drag-and-drop uses pointer events only, context menu is right-click only
- Landing page + gallery have basic Tailwind responsive classes (sm:, lg:, xl:)
- Navbar hides labels on mobile (hidden sm:inline) but nav items disappear entirely (hidden sm:flex)
- No keyboard shortcut platform detection (⌘ shown everywhere, even on Windows)
- Scrollbar styling only WebKit — Firefox on Windows gets no custom scrollbar
- No viewport meta considerations for zoom/scale on touch devices

## Testing
- **iOS/iPad**: User can test directly on own devices
- **Mac browsers**: Safari, Chrome, Firefox (user can test)
- **Windows**: Chrome, Firefox, Edge (need BrowserStack or similar, or a Windows machine)
- **Android**: BrowserStack or Chrome DevTools device emulation for initial pass

## Key Principles
- Desktop-first, mobile-enhanced (not mobile-first)
- The editor is a desktop tool — on mobile, provide a VIEW + CHAT experience, not full editing
- Touch targets: minimum 44px on touch devices (Apple HIG)
- Animations: respect prefers-reduced-motion
- Dark mode + light mode must both look great on all platforms

## Features

### "View Query" on Every Widget

- "Open in Playground" → opens a new playground tab with this query

- Every widget shows a subtle timestamp: "Data as of 2 hours ago"
- Color-coded: green (< 1hr), yellow (1-6hr), red (> 6hr)
- Click → option to force refresh

### Lineage Trail (Advanced)
- For widgets using calculated fields or chained queries:
  - Show the dependency tree: which tables → which joins → which aggregations → final value
  - Collapsible lineage diagram

### Export Widget Data
- Download the widget's underlying data as CSV or JSON
- Download the chart as PNG/SVG
- Copy data to clipboard as TSV (paste into Excel/Google Sheets)

tasks.name: 📐 Visual Query Builder — Sigma-Style No-Code Data Exploration
tasks.notes:
A drag-and-drop query builder for business analysts who know their data deeply but don't write SQL. Inspired by Sigma Computing's spreadsheet-like experience.

## Target Users
Business analysts, ops leads, RevOps — people who live in Sigma or Excel pivot tables. They can build complex analyses but through visual tools, not SQL.

## Core Features

### Column Picker
- Drag columns from the schema sidebar into the query canvas
- Columns auto-grouped by table, searchable
- Shows data type icon (text, number, date, boolean)
- Glossary-linked columns show a ✨ badge

### Visual Operations
- **Group By**: Drag a column to the "Group By" zone
- **Aggregate**: Click a numeric column → pick Sum, Avg, Count, Min, Max, Median
- **Filter**: Click a column → visual filter builder (dropdown for categorical, slider for numeric, date picker for dates)
- **Sort**: Click column header to toggle sort direction
- **Limit**: Slider or input for row limit
- **Join**: Drag a second table in → auto-suggest join condition based on FK relationships

### Formula Bar (Sigma-Style)
- Type expressions like: `Sum([monthly_revenue])`, `CountIf([status] = "active")`
- Formula autocomplete with function reference
- Calculated columns: add a new column with a formula
- Formula reference panel: searchable list of available functions

### Live Preview
- Results update in real-time as the user builds the query
- Performance: debounce 500ms, show loading spinner, cancel previous query
- Row count + estimated query cost shown

### Generated SQL Preview
- "View SQL" toggle shows the SQL being generated under the hood
- Users can copy the SQL and paste it into the SQL Editor for further tweaking
- Helps SQL learners understand what their visual operations translate to

### Save & Wire to Widget
- "Create Widget" button: saves the visual query config and creates a dashboard widget
- Widget stores both the visual query definition AND the generated SQL
- Re-opening the widget in the visual builder restores the drag-and-drop state

## Files

- src/components/data/FormulaBar.tsx — Sigma-style formula input

- src/components/data/FilterBuilder.tsx — visual filter UI

- src/types/visual-query.ts — VisualQueryConfig type definition

## Depends On

- Query Engine (for execution)

tasks.name: 🧪 Query Playground — Interactive Scratch Pad
tasks.notes:
A dedicated space for power users to experiment with queries before wiring them to widgets. Think "Snowflake Worksheets" or "Sigma Workbook" but inside InsightHub.

## Why It's Different From the SQL Editor
The SQL Editor is a panel within the dashboard builder. The Query Playground is a standalone page — a data workbench for exploration, not dashboard construction.

## Features

### Multi-Tab Workspace
- Multiple query tabs open simultaneously (like browser tabs)
- Each tab: editor + results + metadata
- Tabs persist across sessions (saved to DB)

### Side-by-Side Results
- Split view: run two queries and compare results visually
- Useful for verifying: "Does my InsightHub sample data match my Snowflake query?"
- Diff mode: highlight rows/values that differ between two result sets

### Query Chaining (Notebook-Style)
- Chain queries: output of Query 1 becomes input (CTE or temp table) for Query 2
- Add markdown cells between queries for documentation
- Export entire notebook as a shareable artifact

### Data Visualization in Playground
- Quick chart from any result set (one-click: bar, line, scatter)
- Not full widget rendering — just fast exploration charts
- "Promote to Widget" button: takes the query + chart config and creates a proper widget

### Collaboration
- Share a playground session via link
- Comments on specific queries
- "Fork this playground" to create a copy

## Route
- /data/playground — standalone page

tasks.name: 🟣 Power User Data Experience — SQL, Visual Query Builder & Data Verification
tasks.notes:
🟣 POWER USER EXPERIENCE — Data-Savvy Users Epic

## Vision
Make InsightHub a first-class experience for people who live in Snowflake, Sigma, and spreadsheets — not just the "build me a dashboard" AI crowd. These users want to:
- Write or see the actual SQL behind every number
- Verify InsightHub results against their own Snowflake queries
- Build custom formulas and calculated fields (Sigma-style)
- Explore table schemas, preview raw data, understand column types
- Save and share queries like code snippets
- Use a visual query builder when they don't want to write SQL

## Two Power User Personas

### 1. SQL-Native (Data Engineers, Senior Analysts)
- Query Snowflake directly day-to-day
- Want a proper SQL editor with syntax highlighting, autocomplete, schema awareness
- Want to paste their existing Snowflake queries and wire them to widgets
- Trust data only when they can see the query that produced it
- Need: SQL Editor, Query Playground, Data Lineage, Snowflake cross-check

### 2. Sigma-Style (Business Analysts, Ops Leads)
- Comfortable with visual query builders and spreadsheet formulas
- Don't write raw SQL but know their data deeply
- Want: Visual Query Builder, Formula Editor, Pivot Tables, Column Explorer
- Used to expressions like Sum([Revenue]), CountIf([Status] = "active")

## Child Tasks
This epic ties together work across multiple sections:
- 💾 Data Layer: Query Playground, Data Explorer, Snowflake Connector enhancements
- 🔮 Advanced Features: SQL Editor, Visual Query Builder, Calculated Fields
- 🤖 AI & Chat: AI-Assisted SQL, "Explain This Query" features
- 🎨 Canvas & UX: Widget Data Transparency (click-to-see-query)
- 📊 Widget System: SQL-backed widget type, pivot table enhancements

## Guiding Principles
- Every number on screen must be traceable to a query
- Never hide the data layer from someone who wants to see it
- SQL and visual builder are two paths to the same result
- Snowflake is the source of truth — InsightHub should make it easy to verify against
- Permission system (RBAC) gates what data they can query, not the tools they can use

tasks.name: 🔄 Snowflake Cross-Validation Mode
tasks.notes:
For the most skeptical power users: a built-in way to validate that InsightHub's numbers match Snowflake's source of truth.

## The Problem
Power users who query Snowflake directly will NEVER trust a dashboard tool unless they can prove the numbers match. This feature makes verification frictionless.

## Features

### Side-by-Side Validation Panel
- Select any widget → "Validate Against Snowflake" button
- Panel shows:
  - LEFT: InsightHub's value + the query that produced it
  - RIGHT: Input field to paste their own Snowflake query result (or connect live)
  - DIFF: Highlight any discrepancies with explanation of why they might differ

### Auto-Generate Validation Query
- InsightHub generates the exact Snowflake SQL equivalent of the widget's query
- Accounts for:
  - Sample data → production table name mapping
  - Filter differences (date ranges, etc.)
  - Aggregation method differences
- User copies the validation query, runs in Snowflake, pastes result back

### Validation Report
- Run validation across all widgets on a dashboard at once
- Traffic light status: ✅ Match, ⚠️ Close (< 1% diff), ❌ Mismatch
- Export validation report as PDF for audit/compliance
- Timestamp: "Validated on [date] by [user]"

### Scheduled Validation (Phase 4)
- Set up automated daily/weekly validation runs
- Alert if any widget drifts from Snowflake source
- Audit log of all validation results

- src/components/data/ValidationReport.tsx

tasks.name: 🔮 SQL Editor Mode — Full Query Environment for Power Users
tasks.notes:
🔮 SQL Editor Mode — Full-Featured Query Environment for Power Users

## Overview
A proper SQL editor inside InsightHub for users who think in SQL. Not a toy — this should feel like a lightweight version of DataGrip or the Snowflake worksheet UI.

## Target Users
Data engineers, senior analysts, anyone who queries Snowflake directly today. They want to paste their existing queries, validate results, and wire them to dashboard widgets.

## Core Features

### Editor Component
- Monaco Editor (same engine as VS Code) with SQL language mode
- Snowflake SQL dialect support (QUALIFY, FLATTEN, VARIANT, LATERAL, etc.)
- Syntax highlighting, bracket matching, multi-cursor
- Dark theme matching InsightHub's glassmorphism aesthetic

### Schema-Aware Autocomplete
- Table names autocomplete from connected data sources
- Column names autocomplete after typing table alias + dot
- Function autocomplete (Snowflake built-ins: DATE_TRUNC, IFF, ZEROIFNULL, etc.)
- Glossary-aware: typing a metric name suggests the official formula as a snippet
- Schema sidebar: collapsible tree of tables → columns → types

### Query Execution
- "Run" button (⌘+Enter / Ctrl+Enter) executes query
- Results displayed in a high-performance data grid below the editor
- Column sorting, filtering, resizing in the results grid
- Row count, execution time, bytes scanned displayed
- Pagination for large result sets
- Error messages with line/column highlighting

### Query → Widget Pipeline
- "Add to Dashboard" button on any query result
- Auto-detects best widget type from query shape:
  - Single row → KPI card
  - Categorical + numeric → bar chart
  - Time series → line chart
  - Multi-column → table widget
- Saves the SQL as the widget's dataConfig.query field
- Widget re-runs the query on refresh (with caching)

### Query Management
- Save queries with name + description
- Query history (auto-saved, searchable)
- "Fork" a saved query to create a variant
- Share queries with team (respects RBAC permissions)
- Pin frequently-used queries

### Keyboard Shortcuts
- ⌘+Enter: Run query
- ⌘+S: Save query
- ⌘+Shift+F: Format/prettify SQL
- ⌘+/: Toggle comment
- ⌘+D: Duplicate line

## Phase 1 (Sample Data)
- Queries run against in-memory sample data via a lightweight SQL parser (e.g., alasql or sql.js)
- Limited SQL subset: SELECT, FROM, WHERE, GROUP BY, ORDER BY, LIMIT, JOIN, aggregates
- Schema autocomplete from sample data table definitions

## Phase 3 (Snowflake)
- Queries execute against actual Snowflake via the connector
- Full Snowflake SQL dialect support
- Query plan / EXPLAIN output viewer
- Cost estimation (credits consumed)

- src/app/dashboard/[id]/sql-editor/ — SQL editor page (or panel within editor)

tasks.name: 📐 Custom Calculated Fields — Sigma-Style Formulas & SQL Expressions
tasks.notes:
🔮 Custom Calculated Fields — User-Defined Formulas & Computed Metrics

## Overview
Allow power users to create their own computed metrics beyond what the glossary provides. Two interfaces:

### 1. Sigma-Style Formula Language (Primary)
For business analysts who think in spreadsheet formulas:
- Sum([monthly_revenue])
- CountIf([status] = "active")  
- If([churn_rate] > 5, "High Risk", "Healthy")
- DateDiff("month", [start_date], Now())
- RunningTotal([amount], [event_date])
- Percentile([satisfaction_score], 95)

### 2. SQL Expression Mode (Advanced)
For SQL-native users who prefer raw expressions:
- SUM(CASE WHEN status = 'active' THEN monthly_amount ELSE 0 END)
- DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)
- LAG(mrr) OVER (ORDER BY month)

## Features

### Formula Editor
- Inline formula bar (like Excel's formula bar above the grid)
- Autocomplete: column names, functions, glossary terms
- Syntax validation with friendly error messages
- Preview: shows computed result for first 5 rows while typing

### Function Library
Categorized reference panel:
- **Aggregation**: Sum, Avg, Count, CountDistinct, Min, Max, Median, Percentile
- **Conditional**: If, Switch, CountIf, SumIf, Coalesce
- **Date**: DateDiff, DateTrunc, DateAdd, Now, Today, Year, Month, Quarter
- **Text**: Concat, Left, Right, Contains, Replace, Trim, Upper, Lower
- **Window**: RunningTotal, RunningAvg, Rank, RowNumber, Lag, Lead
- **Math**: Round, Abs, Ceil, Floor, Power, Log, Sqrt

### Persistence
- Calculated fields saved per dashboard as part of the schema
- Can be promoted to "shared calculated fields" for reuse across dashboards
- Glossary team can promote a calculated field to an official glossary term

### Type System
- Auto-infer result type from expression
- Type errors caught at edit time ("Cannot compare number to text")
- Null handling: COALESCE defaults, null-safe comparisons

## Files
- src/components/data/FormulaEditor.tsx — inline formula input
- src/components/data/FunctionReference.tsx — searchable function docs
- src/lib/data/formula-parser.ts — parse Sigma-style formulas to AST
- src/lib/data/formula-to-sql.ts — transpile formula AST to SQL
- src/lib/data/formula-evaluator.ts — evaluate formulas against sample data
- src/types/formula.ts — FormulaAST, CalculatedField types

## Depends On
- Schema Explorer (column metadata for autocomplete)
- Visual Query Builder (formula bar integration)
- Query Engine (execution of computed columns)
